title: Puerto Banus Catamaran Rental by the Hour: Costs and Tips for 2026
html:
Renting a catamaran by the hour in Puerto Banus offers a flexible way to explore the Costa del Sol's glamorous coastline, with hourly rates typically ranging from €150 to €400 depending on vessel size and season. BoatHire24 connects you directly with verified local operators for an easy booking experience.

How much does it cost to rent a catamaran by the hour in Puerto Banus?

Hourly catamaran rental in Puerto Banus starts at around €150 for a small 30-foot vessel and rises to €400 for a premium 50-foot model. Prices vary by season, with high season (June, September) commanding a 20, 30% premium. To find the best deal and compare verified boat listings, visit compare verified boat listings on BoatHire24.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

Most catamaran rentals depart from the main marina, Puerto Banus Marina, a purpose-built harbour lined with luxury boutiques and restaurants. Other popular departure points include Marbella Marina (a few minutes east), Estepona Marina (west, 15 nautical miles), and the nearby La Duquesa Marina. You can also head to the Marbella Bay for calm waters or anchor off San Pedro de Alcántara for a coastal view.

Which boat to rent: catamaran or something else?

For hourly rental, catamarans are the top choice for their stability and deck space. Here are four popular options:

  1. Small catamaran (30, 35 ft), Ideal for couples or small families, up to 6 guests, from €180 per hour.
  2. Midsize catamaran (40 ft), Fits 8, 10 guests, perfect for social groups; rates from €250 per hour. See this catamaran for a typical example.
  3. Large catamaran (45, 50 ft), Accommodates up to 12 guests with a skipper, from €350 per hour.
  4. Luxury catamaran (50+ ft), For premium experiences with crew and water toys, from €450 per hour.

Cost breakdown: typical rental prices in Puerto Banus

  • Shared tour (2, 3 hours): from €70 per person
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): from €250
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): from €900
  • Crewed yacht full-day (up to 12 guests): from €1,500

Do you need a licence to rent a catamaran in Puerto Banus?

For bareboat catamaran rental in Spanish waters, a valid ICC (International Certificate of Competence) or equivalent is generally required. However, nearly all hourly rentals include a professional skipper, so no private licence is needed. The operator will confirm specific requirements at booking.

In high season (June, September), the afternoon Poniente breeze can make the sea choppy; book a morning slot for a smoother ride and lighter winds. Many skippers recommend departing by 10:00 to enjoy calm seas and avoid the midday heat.

At a glance

  • Price band: €150, €450 per hour
  • Peak months: June, July, August, September
  • Trip length: 2, 8 hours
  • Capacity: 6, 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can I rent a catamaran without a skipper in Puerto Banus?

Most hourly catamaran rentals come with a skipper, but some operators allow bareboat rental if you hold a valid ICC and pay a deposit.

What is the best time of day for a catamaran trip in Puerto Banus?

Early morning (9:00, 11:00) offers calm seas and cool temperatures; sunset trips (18:00, 20:00) are popular for views and cocktails.

How far can I sail in two hours from Puerto Banus?

You can reach Marbella Bay (about 5 nautical miles east) or Cabopino Beach (7 nautical miles west) at a leisurely pace.

Whether you are planning a birthday celebration or a corporate outing, hourly catamaran rental in Puerto Banus provides prestige and convenience.
